level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required
Expected to engage at implementation level, contributing to architecture decisions
Platform-layer product ownership with cross-team connective tissue responsibilities

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience in agentic AI systems, developer infrastructure, or applied ML products
must_haves.qualifier: in agentic AI systems, developer infrastructure, or applied ML products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep understanding of modern LLM agent architectures, including multi-agent systems, tool-augmented reasoning, memory and retrieval, programmatic orchestration, RAG, and long-horizon execution
Strong grasp of agentic evaluation design, including how to measure task completion, failure recovery, and long-horizon reliability, and how to diagnose model vs. scaffolding gaps

Technically deep enough to contribute to architecture decisions at the implementation level: comfortable reviewing and shaping design docs, reasoning about async execution patterns, sandboxed environments, filesystem design, and the tradeoffs that come with building harness capabilities into a production platform
Ability to flex between ML research conversations and engineering architecture discussions with equal fluency
Track record of shipping platform-layer products with demonstrated impact on reliability, performance, or capability.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: An active practitioner of agent frameworks who regularly builds with and follows the latest developments in open-source harnesses, coding agents, and orchestration tools in both professional and personal work
Hands-on experience with enterprise agentic deployments: multi-tenant orchestration, tool permissioning, audit trails, and compliance requirements

Familiarity with infrastructure constraints relevant to enterprise deployments: on-premises environments, scalability challenges, and the operational tradeoffs of running complex agent workloads in restricted or air-gapped settings
Prior work at the intersection of research and product, translating nascent model capabilities into shipped product features
Background working within or closely alongside an ML research or post-training team

scope.surface: North agent harness — agent loop, context engineering layer, tool orchestration, sandbox execution, sub-agent delegation
core_responsibilities: Define and own the roadmap for North's agent harness, including the agent loop, context engineering layer, tool orchestration, sandbox execution, and sub-agent delegation
Serve as the primary interface between North engineering and Cohere's Modeling team
Engage enterprise customers to surface real-world agentic failures and translate findings into concrete product and model requirements
Stay current with the open-source and commercial agent ecosystem and drive adoption decisions

compensation.equity_form: equity options mentioned as part of compensation package

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface ownership (agent harness, context engineering, model-scaffolding co-evolution)
Encourages candidates to apply even if experience doesn't perfectly align
Explicit equity and competitive compensation mentioned
Generous perks: 6 weeks vacation, full health/dental, parental leave top-up
Hybrid flexibility with multiple global office locations
High-impact role at frontier of agentic AI product development
red_flags: No compensation range disclosed despite equity mention
Role spans three complex domains (agent loop, context engineering, model-scaffolding) — may be unusually broad for one PM
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not addressed in JD
Buzzword density is high; concrete scope of responsibility may be difficult to assess pre-interview
No mention of team size or reporting structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and architecture-level engagement expectations)
scope.business_model (inferred from North platform description as enterprise B2B)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
